item.title: Trump supporter doing Trump supporter things
item.description:
https://www.cbsnews.com/detroit/news/port-huron-man-in-court-for-shooting-of-three-children/
Par for the course. The oldest child who was 17 was killed. The 12 year old daughter is paralyzed. 13-year-old son is now stable condition. I do not understand why this man was not behind bars. I am so tired of these people being able to gaslight and not pay any repercussions for their actions. The fascist administration in America are propping these type of ideals up while squashing the rest of us. Does anybody else think it is sad that these people are such failures that can't even take themselves out of the equation correctly. Front page edit : Well, of course it's one of them, and the stories like these continuously flood out everything, but nothing is done. I have been dealing with building inspectors and really angry landlords. Apparently they did not have a permit for the work they started and have closed some problems by having the building inspectors come out and realize there was some tragedy happening.
item.dc:creator: SquaresNews

item.title: Sure! Give me a bit more context for a unique title
item.description: source: Hi! Could you clarify what you mean by “source”?
item.dc:creator: onushka

item.title: Goodbye baby Scrump
item.description:
On September 11th, Scrump died in my arms
I never wanted to make this post and I absolutely wasn’t expecting to make it when a month ago I was posting about how much I loved and appreciated him and how he was my favorite and a perfect little angel
I’ve told his story before but I’ll tell it again.
From the day he was dropped off on our vet’s counter, there was something wrong with him.
The garbage man who found him said he’d had some type of seizure on the way to the clinic and he was also bleeding from the butt and nose.
The man dropped him off at like 4:50 and we closed at 5 so there wasn’t a whole lot we could do other than give him food and water and keep him comfortable. He fell asleep in his water bowl that night.
When I got to work the next day I got to meet him, somewhat shocked he was still alive. That day I was carrying him around in a towel and he peed on me.
Long story short, he was diagnosed with blindness due to blunt force trauma but I was able to take him home.
The first day I noticed he was obsessed with water and he peed a lot so I brought him in with me the next day for blood work to figure out what was going on.
His blood work and urinalysis came back completely normal. He was still drinking a ton though so we did radiographs and doc first thought there was something wrong with his kidneys but he threw up at the clinic and we were able to see clearly that they looked normal after retaking them.
Doc deduced that it was all neurological because everything kept coming back normal and he had had blunt force trauma.
All the while Scrump just got worse. He started peeing on the bed and on the carpet so we bought him a diaper which arrived the day after he died. We kept testing because I felt sure there was something else going on but it just kept coming back normal.
2 days before the end I noticed he wasn’t eating a whole lot and one of the other doctors told me to bring him in for a shot of convenia to see if it could have been an infection and also to bring in an fecal sample to see if it was parasites.
I was at work so I called my boyfriend to drop him off to do both. This was on September 11th. Chris dropped him off and we offered him food but he didn’t eat at all.
He was fine that morning- he was playing with our other cats, jumping onto his tree
Then my coworker took his temperature and it was dangerously low and I burst into tears, fearing the worst. We ran in-house bloodwork while I called my boyfriend in hysterics to come to the clinic, I feared the worst.
Dr. Hillery pulled me aside while Chris was just running into the building- he was in kidney failure and it did not look good.
My coworker said she never wants to hear the sound that came out of me again- I just wailed while I held him in my arms in a heating pad, trying to warm him up.
Hillery told us to take him home to be with him and keep him warm and check his gum color every so often.
Chris drove me home as I was in hysterics.
Even though I was told the probable prognosis I was hoping beyond hope he would pull through- he was such a fighter.
We went home and wrapped him in blankets and a heating pad while I syringe fed him, trying to get him to come back to us.
But it didn’t work- Scrump was a fighter but this was just too much for him. He was estimated to be around 4 months and we had him for only 6 weeks but he blessed our lives so much.
Even though there was so much heartbreak I’m so glad we were able to be in Scrump’s life, even if it was such a short amount of time.
He was going to be “Forrest Scrump” for Halloween
Big Mama was just starting to warm up to him
The boys were so confused why their favorite little buddy was so sad- it was awful
Please hug your pets tight for us and thank you for reading
item.dc:creator: danat101

item.title: Irish police brutality caught on tape
item.dc:creator: pilomotor

item.title: Empty and blindsided. Mom's gone.
item.description: Can't pull enough together for a detailed post. Dad called me up just before midnight his time to tell me EMS and the police arrived. Mom suffered her first and only heart attack just a couple hours after she fell asleep. She had a class full of 2nd graders that had to learn a horrible life lesson that morning. We're still trying to get everything set up now that Dad's alone and her side of the family is throwing a fit she didn't want a funeral. I just finished writing her obituary. It's a circus.
item.dc:creator: Pokegeologist

item.dc:creator: DownvotesStarWars

item.title: Because Defund the police slogan worked out so well
item.description: I don’t have the right answer to the electoral troubles faced by the democrats. But I’ve seen enough elections to know that you can’t win an election (at any level) by going after an agency that carries a gun. I cut off some friends who claimed to be progressive but lined up behind republicans when defund the police first came about. Nuance doesn’t vote, only slogans do.
item.dc:creator: DownvotesStarWars

item.title: Day 2 of crafting Sauron's Cosplay Armor from LOTR - YuzuPyon
item.description:
Full video : https://youtu.be/dlf6Hj4Hq88 Hello everyone! Tysm for the support on Day 1, if you enjoy this series I will keep posting the daily vlogs on here :) I make slightly longer videos on Youtube if you're interested. The long form videos are still short but have a little bit more informations and I will be working on improving the value of my long format videos in the future! I also just reached 25k subscribers on Youtube, which is amazing I couldn't believe it! It really motivates me to keep creating and sharing my humble knowledge of cosplay making with you! Have a great day everyone, Yuzu
Full video : https://youtu.be/dlf6Hj4Hq88 Hello everyone! Tysm for the support on Day 1, if you enjoy this series I will keep posting the daily vlogs on here :) I make slightly longer videos on Youtube if you're interested. The long form videos are still short but have a little bit more informations and I will be working on improving the value of my long format videos in the future! I also just reached 25k subscribers on Youtube, which is amazing I couldn't believe it! It really motivates me to keep creating and sharing my humble knowledge of cosplay making with you! Have a great day everyone, Yuzu
item.dc:creator: yuzupyon

item.title: Hard truth
item.description: I’m a pastor- I know so many people have been brutalized by the church and by Christians. I am in no way trying to argue or ask folks dismiss the hurt done to them. One reason I preach is because I believe in my God and I take each time preaching as an opportunity to name the hypocrisy and hurt we have done and continue to do. There is a vast difference between what we profess and what we do. As a long term imugurian I’ve shared the insights I’ve read here in church. Thank you all for bravely sharing truth.
item.dc:creator: EmmyZannaT

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1488 (lava lamp chandelier)
item.description:
Let's get this bread. This is the 1,488th post in this series, and I'll be damned if I let those motherfuckers steal a goddamn number for my meme post. I claim this number in the name of Pippin the Glorious.
Pippin tax! Today's message from Pippin: "I know" ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 3) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 4) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride 5) Since multiple people have requested this over the last two weeks or so, my ko-fi is: https://ko-fi.com/codyandpippin
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: GovBrief Today #250
item.description: ? Trump breaks the law. His cabinet enables him. And they'll attack you if you speak up. Troops are now on the ground in Illinois over the governor's objections. Trump says he'll decide which federal workers get their back pay, and OMB deleted the law requiring it from their website. When the Senate Judiciary Committee called in AG Bondi today, she refused to answer questions, instead calling Democratic senators "liar" and "failed lawyer." If they treat senators and governors that way, how are they going to treat you? That's why you need to plan now for which No Kings protest you'll join one week from Saturday on October 18. Until then, don't leave truth to the algorithm. Our free and private nightly email arms you with images, data, and quotes so you can see it all for yourself. Add your email at govbrief dot today or on Substack at Gov Brief Today. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com [1] https://go.govbrief.today/missouri-subpoena-planned-parenthood [2] https://go.govbrief.today/wh-energy-concierge-service [3] https://go.govbrief.today/senate-confirms-neil-jacobs [4] https://go.govbrief.today/noem-visit-ice-portland [5] https://go.govbrief.today/texas-guard-in-illinois [6] https://go.govbrief.today/vermont-social-media-center [7] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-immigration-courts-head [8] https://go.govbrief.today/pam-bondi-senate-testimony [9] https://go.govbrief.today/phone-records-lawmakers-jan6 [10] https://go.govbrief.today/senate-confirms-dozens-nominees [11] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-back-pay-workers [12] https://go.govbrief.today/atc-flight-delays-sickout [13] https://go.govbrief.today/zach-bryan-song-ice Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: A fitting tribute
item.description: Sir David Attenborough's heartfelt farewell to Jane Goodall: "The world is a quieter place without her" At Dr. Jane Goodall's funeral, Sir David Attenborough delivered words that forever defined the end of an era. Standing before a sea of ​​mourners, the beloved naturalist called Jane "the conscience of conservation" as he paid tribute to his lifelong friend and trailblazer. Sir David, his voice trembling, spoke of six decades of friendship, shared adventures, and a steadfast belief in the power of hope. He said, movingly: "The world is a quieter place without her, but her spirit still whispers in the trees." It was more than a farewell — it was a promise that Jane's legacy will continue to guide those who listen to the voice of nature.
item.dc:creator: trumpypumpyinyourrumpy
